The AI threat research text highlights the potential security risks associated with large-scale pre-trained models in machine intelligence. The specific threat discussed is called the "Neuron-level backdoor attack" (NeuBA), which involves embedding a backdoor trigger in a pre-trained model that can be activated later to produce malicious output.

The attack mechanism works as follows:

1. The attacker embeds a trigger in a subset of the training data, which is used to fine-tune the pre-trained model.
